summary: Closed, unmerged SysDolphin baselib matching-improvement slice split from a larger branch. The slice targeted src/sysdolphin/baselib and mainly adjusted string/data relocation handling and small code-shape details in particle.c, with minor matching tweaks in cobj.c, hsd_3B34.c, sislib.c, and synth.c. It was superseded by PR #2632, which combined Dolphin support changes for easier review and CI.
what_changed: Added PAD_STACK(4) in HSD_CObjSetUpVector in cobj.c, likely to tune stack layout for matching.
Reordered local assignments in hsd_803B46D4 so sp8/spC loads from lbl_804DEB88 occur before assigning jmp_buf and temp_r4.
In particle.c, introduced a static byte array lbl_8040A540 containing packed MCC error/report strings and replaced many direct string literals in fn_80392CD8 with offsets into that array.
Changed MCC error OSReport format strings in fn_80392CD8 to use lbl_8040A540 offsets instead of inline literals.
Added extern references to lbl_8040A8E0 and lbl_8040A904 and used them for hsd_803931A4 EXI/MCC initialization OSReport messages.
Changed Exception_ReportStackTrace and Exception_ReportCodeline to reference strings through lbl_8040AB00 offsets, matching known TODO notes about string relocation differences.
Simplified case 6 handling in hsd_80395D88 by accessing sp->xD4 directly instead of through a temporary OSContext** ctx_ptr.
Changed sislib.c OSPanic empty-string arguments from literal "" to (char*) HSD_SisLib_804D6390 in three allocation error paths.
Changed a synth.c sentinel comparison from arithmetic cast form to a direct signed constant comparison: x8 == -0x10001.
The PR body reports this was an isolated path slice created from origin/master and that git diff --check passed; full unsplit source branch had green CI before splitting.
smart_moves: For string relocation mismatches, the patch grouped adjacent literal data into label-based byte arrays and used explicit offsets, preserving exact data layout rather than relying on compiler literal pooling.
The particle.c changes target functions already annotated as near-matches with relocation differences, making the slice narrowly focused on known matching blockers.
Using extern labels such as lbl_8040AB00, lbl_8040A8E0, and lbl_8040A904 allowed code to reuse existing data addresses instead of emitting duplicate string literals.
Small expression rewrites, such as removing ctx_ptr indirection and changing the synth sentinel comparison, show codegen-focused tuning without changing apparent behavior.
The author split the larger branch by directory/subsystem to make matching improvements easier to review, and closed this PR once a better combined Dolphin support split was created.
decomp_lessons: When a function's instructions match but relocation encoding differs, inspect whether string literals need to be represented as a shared labeled blob with manual offsets.
Direct string literals can be semantically clearer, but exact matching may require label-plus-offset references to reproduce original rodata layout and relocations.
Near-match TODOs should document the exact remaining issue, as seen in comments noting BSS relocation or string relocation differences.
Small source-order changes in local assignments can affect register allocation or load scheduling enough to matter for matching.
Stack padding macros such as PAD_STACK can be appropriate for isolated stack-frame discrepancies, but should be localized to the affected function.
Replacing temporary pointer-to-pointer locals with direct struct-field access can alter generated code while preserving behavior; this is a useful matching tactic to test.
Path-sliced PRs help review focused decomp improvements, but related support changes may be better combined when review and CI need shared context.
naming_conventions: Unidentified data labels use lbl_XXXXXXXX names, for example lbl_8040A540, lbl_8040A8E0, lbl_8040A904, and lbl_8040AB00.
Unidentified or provisional functions retain fn_XXXXXXXX or hsd_XXXXXXXX names, for example fn_80392CD8, hsd_803931A4, and hsd_80395D88.
SysDolphin public-style baselib symbols use HSD_ prefixes, for example HSD_CObjSetUpVector, HSD_SisLib_803A5798, and HSD_Synth_8038ADD0.
Global data labels in this area use subsystem-prefixed HSD_*_804D* names when partially identified, for example HSD_SisLib_804D6390.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use PAD_STACK(n) to force stack-frame shape when normal declarations do not produce the expected frame.
Represent packed rodata/string tables as explicit byte arrays when the compiler's normal string literal emission produces wrong relocations.
Reference string tables via base label plus constant offsets to match original relocations.
Use extern declarations for already-existing rodata labels to avoid duplicate literals and steer relocations to the expected address.
Try reordering independent local assignments to influence emitted load/store order.
Try direct field access instead of pointer-to-pointer temporaries when the extra local changes register or memory access codegen.
Rewrite constant comparisons into equivalent signed/unsigned forms to match the original instruction sequence.
Use shared empty-string storage for panic/report arguments when literal "" produces the wrong data reference.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the provided context.
Issue comment states the PR was superseded by combined Dolphin support split PR #2632, which keeps support changes together for review and CI.
The PR was closed without merge.
follow_up_queries: Check PR #2632 for the merged or reviewed version of these Dolphin support matching changes.
Search for remaining TODO comments mentioning string relocation differences in src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c.
Audit whether lbl_8040A540 and lbl_8040AB00 offsets were later replaced with named string table structures or kept as raw labels.
Compare the final merged diff from #2632 against this closed slice to see which tactics survived review.
